<style>
  :root{
    --bg:#0f1218; --panel:#161b24; --panel2:#1c2230;
    --ink:#e8ecf1; --mute:#9aa4b2; --line:#2a3242;
    --yellow:#ffd866; --blue:#58c4dd; --green:#83c167;
    --pink:#e07a5f; --violet:#b39ddb; --cyan:#7de0d6;
  }
  *{box-sizing:border-box}
  html,body{margin:0;padding:0;background:var(--bg);color:var(--ink);
    font-family:-apple-system,BlinkMacSystemFont,"Segoe UI",Inter,Roboto,sans-serif;
    font-size:17px;line-height:1.65}
  a{color:var(--blue)}
  code{font-family:ui-monospace,SFMono-Regular,Menlo,Consolas,monospace;
    background:#0b0f16;padding:1px 6px;border-radius:4px;color:#f0c987;font-size:0.92em}
  main{max-width:880px;margin:0 auto;padding:4rem 1.5rem 8rem}
  h1{font-size:2.4rem;letter-spacing:-0.01em;margin:0 0 0.4rem}
  h2{font-size:1.6rem;margin:3.2rem 0 0.8rem;color:#fff;border-bottom:1px solid var(--line);padding-bottom:.4rem}
  h3{font-size:1.15rem;margin:1.8rem 0 .6rem;color:var(--yellow)}
  p{margin:0.7rem 0}
  .sub{color:var(--mute);margin-top:0;margin-bottom:2rem}
  .widget{background:var(--panel);border:1px solid var(--line);border-radius:10px;
    padding:1rem 1.1rem 1.2rem;margin:1.2rem 0}
  .widget .hd{display:flex;justify-content:space-between;align-items:baseline;margin-bottom:.6rem;gap:.8rem;flex-wrap:wrap}
  .widget .hd .ttl{font-weight:600;color:#fff;font-size:.95rem;letter-spacing:.02em;text-transform:uppercase}
  .widget .hd .hint{color:var(--mute);font-size:.85rem}
  .row{display:flex;gap:.6rem;flex-wrap:wrap;align-items:center;margin:.4rem 0}
  .row label{color:var(--mute);font-size:.88rem}
  input[type="text"],input[type="number"],select{
    background:#0b0f16;color:var(--ink);border:1px solid var(--line);
    border-radius:6px;padding:.35rem .55rem;font-family:inherit;font-size:.95rem}
  input[type="range"]{accent-color:var(--yellow);flex:1;max-width:260px}
  button{background:var(--panel2);color:var(--ink);border:1px solid var(--line);
    border-radius:6px;padding:.35rem .7rem;cursor:pointer;font:inherit}
  button:hover{background:#242c3e}
  button.active{background:var(--yellow);color:#111;border-color:var(--yellow)}
  .readout{background:#0b0f16;border:1px solid var(--line);border-radius:6px;
    padding:.6rem .8rem;font-family:ui-monospace,monospace;font-size:.92rem;color:#cbd5e1;
    min-height:1.6em;white-space:pre-wrap;word-break:break-word}
  .pill{display:inline-block;background:var(--panel2);border:1px solid var(--line);
    border-radius:999px;padding:1px 8px;font-size:.82rem;color:var(--mute);margin-right:4px}
  .note{border-left:3px solid var(--violet);padding:.3rem .9rem;color:#d8d2ef;background:#1a1828;
    margin:1rem 0;border-radius:0 6px 6px 0}
  .ok{border-left:3px solid var(--green);padding:.3rem .9rem;color:#d5ebc8;background:#1a2418;
    margin:1rem 0;border-radius:0 6px 6px 0}
  .bad{border-left:3px solid var(--pink);padding:.3rem .9rem;color:#f4d2c7;background:#2a1a18;
    margin:1rem 0;border-radius:0 6px 6px 0}
  svg{display:block;margin:0 auto;max-width:100%}
  svg text{fill:var(--ink);font-family:inherit}
  nav.toc{position:sticky;top:0;background:rgba(15,18,24,.93);backdrop-filter:blur(8px);
    border-bottom:1px solid var(--line);padding:.6rem 1rem;z-index:10;
    display:flex;gap:1rem;flex-wrap:wrap;font-size:.9rem}
  nav.toc a{color:var(--mute);text-decoration:none}
  nav.toc a:hover{color:var(--yellow)}
  /* sticky sidebar TOC (wide screens only; narrow screens fall back to top-nav) */
  aside.sidetoc{position:fixed;top:4.2rem;
    left:max(1rem, calc(50% - 440px - 220px));
    width:200px;max-height:calc(100vh - 6rem);overflow-y:auto;
    padding-right:1rem;border-right:1px solid var(--line);
    font-size:.88rem;z-index:5}
  aside.sidetoc .sidetoc-title{font-size:.7rem;letter-spacing:.14em;text-transform:uppercase;
    color:var(--mute);margin:0 0 .5rem;padding-bottom:.35rem;border-bottom:1px solid var(--line)}
  aside.sidetoc ol{list-style:none;padding:0;margin:0}
  aside.sidetoc li{margin:0}
  aside.sidetoc a{display:block;padding:.3rem .5rem;color:var(--mute);text-decoration:none;
    border-left:2px solid transparent;line-height:1.35;
    transition:color 120ms ease, border-color 120ms ease}
  aside.sidetoc a:hover{color:var(--ink)}
  aside.sidetoc a.active{color:var(--yellow);border-left-color:var(--yellow)}
  @media (max-width:1180px){ aside.sidetoc{display:none} }
  @media (min-width:1181px){ nav.toc a[href^="#"]{display:none} }
  .hero{padding:3rem 0 1rem}
  .small{font-size:.88rem;color:var(--mute)}
  footer{color:var(--mute);font-size:.88rem;text-align:center;margin-top:4rem}
  table.plain{width:100%;border-collapse:collapse;font-size:.95rem;margin:.6rem 0}
  table.plain th,table.plain td{padding:.35rem .5rem;border-bottom:1px solid var(--line);text-align:left}
  table.plain th{color:var(--mute);font-weight:500}
  /* callback-css-auto-begin */
  aside.callback{
    margin:1.2rem 0;padding:.7rem 1rem;
    background:rgba(88,196,221,0.05);
    border-left:3px solid color-mix(in srgb, var(--cyan) 45%, transparent);
    border-radius:0 6px 6px 0;
    font-size:.93rem;
  }
  aside.callback .ttl{
    font-size:.68rem;letter-spacing:.14em;text-transform:uppercase;
    color:var(--mute,#8c9aa6);margin-bottom:.3rem;font-weight:600;
  }
  aside.callback ul{margin:0;padding-left:1.1rem}
  aside.callback ul li{margin:.15rem 0}
  aside.callback a{color:inherit}
/* callback-css-auto-end */
  details.changelog{
    margin:2rem 0 1rem;padding:.6rem .9rem;
    background:rgba(0,0,0,0.22);
    border-radius:6px;
    font-size:.85rem;
  }
  details.changelog summary{
    cursor:pointer;letter-spacing:.1em;text-transform:uppercase;
    font-size:.7rem;color:var(--mute,#8c9aa6);
  }
  details.changelog table{margin-top:.5rem;border-collapse:collapse;width:100%;font-size:.82rem}
  details.changelog td{padding:.2rem .6rem;border-bottom:1px dashed rgba(255,255,255,0.07);vertical-align:top}
  details.changelog td:first-child{color:var(--mute,#8c9aa6);white-space:nowrap;font-variant-numeric:tabular-nums}
  aside.related{
    margin:1.2rem 0;padding:.7rem 1rem;
    background:color-mix(in srgb, var(--mute) 6%, transparent);
    border-left:3px solid color-mix(in srgb, var(--mute) 55%, transparent);
    border-radius:0 6px 6px 0;
    font-size:.93rem;
  }
  aside.related .ttl{
    font-size:.68rem;letter-spacing:.14em;text-transform:uppercase;
    color:var(--mute,#8c9aa6);margin-bottom:.3rem;font-weight:600;
  }
  aside.related > div{margin:.15rem 0}
  aside.related .more{color:var(--mute);font-style:italic}
  aside.related a{color:inherit}

  html[data-hide-widgets] .widget { display: none !important; }
  html[data-hide-quizzes] .quiz { display: none !important; }
  html[data-hide-lineage] .lineage-strip { display: none !important; }
  .mv-display-toggles { display: inline-flex; gap: .6rem; align-items: center; }

  .mv-display-toggle--off { opacity: 0.35; }

← Notebook
1 Itô integral 2 Itô's formula 3 SDEs 4 Girsanov 5 Feynman–Kac 6 Applications

Stochastic calculus

Brownian motion has infinite total variation but finite quadratic variation $[B]_t=t$ — classical Riemann–Stieltjes calculus breaks, and a new integral is required. The Itô integral chooses the left endpoint, gains a martingale, and pays in a chain rule with a curvature correction. Out of this come SDEs, Girsanov's drift change, Feynman–Kac's PDE bridge, and the toolbox behind Black–Scholes, the Kalman filter, and optimal stopping.

1. The Itô integral

Brownian motion's paths are continuous but nowhere differentiable, with quadratic variation $\sum (B_{t_{k+1}}-B_{t_k})^2\to t$ in $L^2$ as the partition $\{t_k\}$ refines. That blows up the total variation, so $\int H_s\,dB_s$ cannot be defined pathwise as a Riemann–Stieltjes integral. Itô's idea: build the integral in $L^2$ by approximation on simple processes, then choose an evaluation rule that keeps the integrand predictable.

Simple processes. A process is simple if it has the form $H_s=\sum_{k=0}^{n-1}\xi_k\mathbf{1}_{(t_k,t_{k+1}]}(s)$ with $\xi_k$ being $\mathcal{F}_{t_k}$-measurable and bounded. For such $H$, define $$\int_0^t H_s\,dB_s\;=\;\sum_{k=0}^{n-1}\xi_k\,(B_{t_{k+1}\wedge t}-B_{t_k\wedge t}).$$ The key fact is the Itô isometry: $\mathbb{E}\big[(\int_0^t H_s\,dB_s)^2\big]=\mathbb{E}\int_0^t H_s^2\,ds$. This makes the simple-process map $H\mapsto\int H\,dB$ an isometry from $L^2(\Omega\times[0,T],ds\,d\mathbb{P})$ into $L^2(\mathbb{P})$, and density of simple processes in this $L^2$ extends the integral uniquely.

The result is a martingale. Predictability of $H$ is what kills drift: $\mathbb{E}\big[\xi_k(B_{t_{k+1}}-B_{t_k})\mid\mathcal{F}_{t_k}\big]=\xi_k\cdot\mathbb{E}[B_{t_{k+1}}-B_{t_k}\mid\mathcal{F}_{t_k}]=0$. So $\int_0^t H_s\,dB_s$ is a continuous $L^2$-martingale.

Itô vs. Stratonovich. Choosing the midpoint $\tfrac{1}{2}(H_{t_k}+H_{t_{k+1}})$ yields the Stratonovich integral $\int H\circ dB$. It obeys the classical chain rule, but is no longer a martingale; the right-endpoint convention ($H_{t_{k+1}}$) is even worse — it correlates with the increment and adds a drift. The widget below lets you compute $\int_0^t B_s\,dB_s$ under each convention on a single Brownian path and see the gap converging to $\pm t/2$.

Riemann sums for $\int_0^t B_s\,dB_s$

<script>
/* §1 — Itô vs Stratonovich vs right-endpoint Riemann sums */
(function(){
  const svg=$('#ito-svg'), out=$('#ito-out');
  const NIn=$('#ito-N'), NV=$('#ito-Nv');
  const W=760, H=320, x0=60, x1=W-30, y0=30, y1=H-40;
  const T=1.0, M=2000; // master fine grid for path
  let path=null;
  function genPath(){
    const dt=T/M, B=new Float64Array(M+1);
    for(let i=1;i<=M;i++) B[i]=B[i-1]+Math.sqrt(dt)*randn();
    path=B;
  }
  function sums(N){
    // sample path at N+1 points evenly
    const idx=k=>Math.round(k*M/N);
    const Bs=new Array(N+1);
    for(let k=0;k<=N;k++) Bs[k]=path[idx(k)];
    let SL=0, SM=0, SR=0;
    for(let k=0;k<N;k++){
      const dB=Bs[k+1]-Bs[k];
      SL += Bs[k]*dB;
      SM += 0.5*(Bs[k]+Bs[k+1])*dB;
      SR += Bs[k+1]*dB;
    }
    return {SL,SM,SR,Bs};
  }
  function draw(){
    const N=+NIn.value; NV.textContent='N = '+N;
    const {SL,SM,SR,Bs}=sums(N);
    svg.innerHTML='';
    // axes
    let bmin=Infinity, bmax=-Infinity;
    for(const v of Bs){ if(v<bmin)bmin=v; if(v>bmax)bmax=v; }
    const pad=0.3;
    bmin-=pad; bmax+=pad;
    const X=k=>x0 + (k/N)*(x1-x0);
    const Y=v=>y1 - (v-bmin)/(bmax-bmin)*(y1-y0);
    svg.appendChild(SVG('line',{x1:x0,y1:y1,x2:x1,y2:y1,stroke:'var(--line)'}));
    svg.appendChild(SVG('line',{x1:x0,y1:y0,x2:x0,y2:y1,stroke:'var(--line)'}));
    // zero
    if(bmin<0 && bmax>0){
      const yz=Y(0);
      svg.appendChild(SVG('line',{x1:x0,y1:yz,x2:x1,y2:yz,stroke:'var(--mute)','stroke-dasharray':'2 4'}));
    }
    // path polyline
    let d='';
    for(let k=0;k<=N;k++){ d += (k===0?'M':'L')+X(k)+','+Y(Bs[k])+' '; }
    svg.appendChild(SVG('path',{d,fill:'none',stroke:'var(--yellow)','stroke-width':1.5}));
    // little dots at sample points (downsample if N huge)
    const showDots = N<=120;
    if(showDots){
      for(let k=0;k<=N;k++){
        svg.appendChild(SVG('circle',{cx:X(k),cy:Y(Bs[k]),r:1.8,fill:'var(--yellow)'}));
      }
    }
    // overlay rectangles: left-endpoint blocks (Itô)
    for(let k=0;k<N;k++){
      const xa=X(k), xb=X(k+1);
      const va=Bs[k];
      const yV=Y(va), yZ=Y(0);
      const yTop=Math.min(yV,yZ), yBot=Math.max(yV,yZ);
      svg.appendChild(SVG('rect',{x:xa,y:yTop,width:Math.max(0,xb-xa),height:Math.max(0,yBot-yTop),
        fill: va>=0 ? 'rgba(125,224,214,0.10)' : 'rgba(224,122,95,0.10)',
        stroke:'var(--cyan)','stroke-width':0.4}));
    }
    // title
    const cap=SVG('text',{x:W/2,y:20,'text-anchor':'middle','font-size':12,fill:'var(--mute)'});
    cap.textContent = `Brownian path Bₜ on [0,1] · rectangles use left endpoint (Itô) — same widths, different heights for L/M/R`;
    svg.appendChild(cap);
    // limits annotation
    const limit = 0.5*(Bs[N]*Bs[N] - T); // = SL by Itô
    out.textContent =
      `Sample sums for ∫₀¹ Bₛ dBₛ at N = ${N}\n`+
      `  Itô      (left)     SL = ${SL.toFixed(4)}\n`+
      `  Stratonovich (mid)  SM = ${SM.toFixed(4)}\n`+
      `  right endpoint      SR = ${SR.toFixed(4)}\n\n`+
      `expected limits as N→∞ on this path:\n`+
      `  SL → (B_T² − T)/2  = ${limit.toFixed(4)}   (Itô)\n`+
      `  SM → SL + T/2      = ${(limit+T/2).toFixed(4)}   (Stratonovich)\n`+
      `  SR → SL + T        = ${(limit+T).toFixed(4)}     (drift = T)`;
  }
  $('#ito-new').addEventListener('click',()=>{genPath(); draw();});
  NIn.addEventListener('input', draw);
  genPath(); draw();
})();
</script>
</section>

2. Itô's formula

For a $C^2$ function $f\colon\mathbb{R}\to\mathbb{R}$ and an Itô process $X_t$ with $dX_t=b_t\,dt+\sigma_t\,dB_t$, Itô's formula states $$df(X_t)=f'(X_t)\,dX_t+\tfrac{1}{2}f''(X_t)\,(dX_t)^2,\qquad (dX_t)^2=\sigma_t^2\,dt.$$ The chain rule of ordinary calculus is the first term; the second term is the Itô correction, a Taylor remainder $\tfrac{1}{2}f''(X)\,d\langle X\rangle$ that is invisible for smooth $X$ but unavoidable when the quadratic variation is non-zero.

The pedagogical case: $f(x)=x^2$, $X_t=B_t$. Naive calculus would say $d(B_t^2)=2B_t\,dB_t$ and conclude $B_t^2=2\int_0^t B_s\,dB_s$. Itô gives $$d(B_t^2)=2B_t\,dB_t+dt\quad\Longrightarrow\quad B_t^2 = 2\int_0^t B_s\,dB_s + t.$$ The discrepancy is exactly the quadratic variation: along any partition $\sum(B_{t_{k+1}}-B_{t_k})^2\to t$. The widget below plots $B_t^2$ in yellow against the naive prediction $2\int_0^t B_s\,dB_s$ in pink — the gap fills in to $t$ as time advances.

Time-dependent version. For $f(t,x)\in C^{1,2}$, $$df(t,X_t)=\partial_t f\,dt+\partial_x f\,dX_t+\tfrac{1}{2}\partial_{xx} f\,(dX_t)^2.$$ This is what makes the exponential martingale work: applying Itô to $f(t,B)=\exp(\theta B - \tfrac{1}{2}\theta^2 t)$ gives $df=\theta f\,dB$, i.e. zero drift. Time-dependent $f$ is also what links SDEs to PDEs in §5.

Mnemonic: $(dB)^2=dt$, $dt\,dB=0$, $(dt)^2=0$. All Itô calculations reduce to a Taylor expansion using these multiplication rules. The first is the only one that's surprising; it's the heart of stochastic calculus.

$B_t^2$ vs. naive $2\int B\,dB$
gap = $t$ = quadratic variation

<script>
/* §2 — Itô's formula via B_t² versus 2∫B dB */
(function(){
  const svg=$('#itof-svg'), out=$('#itof-out');
  const KIn=$('#itof-K'), KV=$('#itof-Kv');
  const W=760, H=320, x0=60, x1=W-30, y0=30, y1=H-40;
  const T=1.0;
  let B=null, K0=2000;
  function genPath(K){
    const dt=T/K; const arr=new Float64Array(K+1);
    for(let i=1;i<=K;i++) arr[i]=arr[i-1]+Math.sqrt(dt)*randn();
    return arr;
  }
  function compute(){
    const K=+KIn.value; KV.textContent='K = '+K;
    if(!B || B.length-1!==K) B = genPath(K);
    const dt=T/K;
    const sq=new Float64Array(K+1);
    const I=new Float64Array(K+1); // 2*∫B dB cumulative
    for(let i=1;i<=K;i++){
      sq[i]=B[i]*B[i];
      I[i]=I[i-1] + 2*B[i-1]*(B[i]-B[i-1]);
    }
    return {K, sq, I};
  }
  function draw(){
    const {K, sq, I}=compute();
    svg.innerHTML='';
    // y range
    let mn=Infinity, mx=-Infinity;
    for(let i=0;i<=K;i++){
      if(sq[i]<mn)mn=sq[i]; if(sq[i]>mx)mx=sq[i];
      if(I[i]<mn)mn=I[i]; if(I[i]>mx)mx=I[i];
    }
    if(mn>0)mn=0;
    if(mx<T)mx=T;
    const pad=(mx-mn)*0.08+0.05; mn-=pad; mx+=pad;
    const X=i=>x0 + (i/K)*(x1-x0);
    const Y=v=>y1 - (v-mn)/(mx-mn)*(y1-y0);
    svg.appendChild(SVG('line',{x1:x0,y1:y1,x2:x1,y2:y1,stroke:'var(--line)'}));
    svg.appendChild(SVG('line',{x1:x0,y1:y0,x2:x0,y2:y1,stroke:'var(--line)'}));
    // zero
    if(mn<0&&mx>0){ const yz=Y(0); svg.appendChild(SVG('line',{x1:x0,y1:yz,x2:x1,y2:yz,stroke:'var(--mute)','stroke-dasharray':'2 4'})); }
    // y = t reference line (the gap)
    let dt='';
    for(let i=0;i<=K;i++){ const t=i/K*T; dt += (i===0?'M':'L')+X(i)+','+Y(t)+' '; }
    svg.appendChild(SVG('path',{d:dt,fill:'none',stroke:'var(--cyan)','stroke-width':1.2,'stroke-dasharray':'4 3'}));
    // 2*∫B dB
    let dI='';
    for(let i=0;i<=K;i++){ dI += (i===0?'M':'L')+X(i)+','+Y(I[i])+' '; }
    svg.appendChild(SVG('path',{d:dI,fill:'none',stroke:'var(--pink)','stroke-width':1.4}));
    // B^2
    let ds='';
    for(let i=0;i<=K;i++){ ds += (i===0?'M':'L')+X(i)+','+Y(sq[i])+' '; }
    svg.appendChild(SVG('path',{d:ds,fill:'none',stroke:'var(--yellow)','stroke-width':1.6}));
    // legend (viewport coords)
    const lg=SVG('g',{transform:'translate(80,40)'});
    function le(y,col,lab){
      lg.appendChild(SVG('line',{x1:0,y1:y,x2:18,y2:y,stroke:col,'stroke-width':2}));
      const t=SVG('text',{x:24,y:y+4,'font-size':12,fill:col}); t.textContent=lab; lg.appendChild(t);
    }
    le(0,'var(--yellow)','B²ₜ (Itô prediction)');
    le(16,'var(--pink)','2 ∫ Bₛ dBₛ (naive)');
    le(32,'var(--cyan)','y = t (the Itô correction)');
    svg.appendChild(lg);
    // x ticks
    for(let k=0;k<=4;k++){
      const xp=X(k*K/4);
      svg.appendChild(SVG('line',{x1:xp,y1:y1,x2:xp,y2:y1+4,stroke:'var(--mute)'}));
      const t=SVG('text',{x:xp,y:y1+18,'text-anchor':'middle','font-size':10,fill:'var(--mute)'});
      t.textContent=(k/4).toFixed(2); svg.appendChild(t);
    }
    const cap=SVG('text',{x:W/2,y:20,'text-anchor':'middle','font-size':12,fill:'var(--mute)'});
    cap.textContent='time t ∈ [0,1]';
    svg.appendChild(cap);
    // readout
    const i=K, gap=sq[i]-I[i];
    out.textContent =
      `at t = 1.000 on this path:\n`+
      `  B² = ${sq[i].toFixed(4)}\n`+
      `  2 ∫ B dB = ${I[i].toFixed(4)}\n`+
      `  gap = ${gap.toFixed(4)}   (theory: t = 1.000)\n\n`+
      `the gap ≈ t holds path-by-path (it's the quadratic variation, deterministic).`;
  }
  $('#itof-new').addEventListener('click',()=>{B=null; draw();});
  KIn.addEventListener('input',()=>{B=null; draw();});
  draw();
})();
</script>
</section>

3. Stochastic differential equations

An SDE writes a process as the sum of a drift and a diffusion: $$dX_t=b(t,X_t)\,dt+\sigma(t,X_t)\,dB_t,\qquad X_0=x_0.$$ Solutions come in two flavours. A strong solution is a process $X$ adapted to the filtration of a given Brownian motion $B$ that satisfies the integrated form $X_t=x_0+\int_0^t b\,ds+\int_0^t\sigma\,dB_s$. A weak solution only requires producing some probability space carrying $(X,B)$ jointly satisfying the equation in law — the driving Brownian motion is constructed along with the solution.

Picard iteration and Lipschitz uniqueness. If $b,\sigma$ are globally Lipschitz with linear growth, the operator $T(X)_t=x_0+\int_0^t b(s,X_s)\,ds+\int_0^t\sigma(s,X_s)\,dB_s$ is a contraction in $L^2$ on $[0,T]$ (use Itô isometry on the diffusion piece, Cauchy–Schwarz on the drift). Iterating gives a unique strong solution; the existence proof is parallel to ODE Picard, with the Itô isometry replacing $|\int|\le\int|\,|$.

Geometric Brownian motion. The canonical financial example is $dX_t=\mu X_t\,dt+\sigma X_t\,dB_t$. Apply Itô to $\log X_t$: with $f(x)=\log x$, $f'=1/x$, $f''=-1/x^2$, $$d\log X_t=\frac{1}{X_t}\,dX_t-\frac{1}{2X_t^2}\,(dX_t)^2 = \mu\,dt+\sigma\,dB_t-\tfrac{1}{2}\sigma^2\,dt = (\mu-\tfrac{1}{2}\sigma^2)\,dt+\sigma\,dB_t.$$ Integrating, $X_t=x_0\exp((\mu-\sigma^2/2)t+\sigma B_t)$. Two surprises live here: (1) $X_t$ stays positive a.s.; (2) $\mathbb{E}[X_t]=x_0 e^{\mu t}$ but the median grows like $x_0 e^{(\mu-\sigma^2/2)t}$ — the lognormal mean–median gap is a real phenomenon, "volatility drag".

<script>
/* §3 — geometric Brownian motion ensemble */
(function(){
  const svg=$('#gbm-svg'), out=$('#gbm-out');
  const muIn=$('#gbm-mu'), sigIn=$('#gbm-sig'), KIn=$('#gbm-K');
  const muV=$('#gbm-muv'), sigV=$('#gbm-sigv'), KV=$('#gbm-Kv');
  const W=760, H=320, x0=60, x1=W-30, y0=30, y1=H-40;
  const T=2.0, N=400, x_init=1.0;
  let seed=null;
  function genSeeds(K){
    const arr=[];
    for(let k=0;k<K;k++){
      const z=new Float64Array(N);
      for(let i=0;i<N;i++) z[i]=randn();
      arr.push(z);
    }
    return arr;
  }
  function path(z, mu, sig){
    const dt=T/N; const X=new Float64Array(N+1); X[0]=x_init;
    for(let i=1;i<=N;i++){
      // exact: X_i = X_{i-1} * exp((mu-sig^2/2) dt + sig sqrt(dt) z_{i-1})
      X[i] = X[i-1]*Math.exp((mu-0.5*sig*sig)*dt + sig*Math.sqrt(dt)*z[i-1]);
    }
    return X;
  }
  function draw(){
    const mu=+muIn.value, sig=+sigIn.value, K=+KIn.value;
    muV.textContent='μ = '+mu.toFixed(2);
    sigV.textContent='σ = '+sig.toFixed(2);
    KV.textContent='K = '+K;
    if(!seed || seed.length<K) seed=genSeeds(Math.max(K,50));
    const paths=[];
    for(let k=0;k<K;k++) paths.push(path(seed[k], mu, sig));
    svg.innerHTML='';
    let mn=Infinity, mx=-Infinity;
    for(const p of paths) for(const v of p){ if(v<mn)mn=v; if(v>mx)mx=v; }
    mn=Math.max(0, mn-0.1*(mx-mn));
    mx=mx+0.1*(mx-mn);
    const X=i=>x0 + (i/N)*(x1-x0);
    const Y=v=>y1 - (v-mn)/(mx-mn)*(y1-y0);
    // axes
    svg.appendChild(SVG('line',{x1:x0,y1:y1,x2:x1,y2:y1,stroke:'var(--line)'}));
    svg.appendChild(SVG('line',{x1:x0,y1:y0,x2:x0,y2:y1,stroke:'var(--line)'}));
    // ensemble paths in muted colour
    for(let k=0;k<K;k++){
      let d='';
      for(let i=0;i<=N;i++){ d += (i===0?'M':'L')+X(i)+','+Y(paths[k][i])+' '; }
      svg.appendChild(SVG('path',{d,fill:'none',stroke:'var(--violet)','stroke-width':0.7,'stroke-opacity':0.55}));
    }
    // theoretical mean curve  E[X_t] = x0 exp(mu t)
    let dm=''; for(let i=0;i<=N;i++){ const t=i/N*T; dm+=(i===0?'M':'L')+X(i)+','+Y(x_init*Math.exp(mu*t))+' '; }
    svg.appendChild(SVG('path',{d:dm,fill:'none',stroke:'var(--pink)','stroke-width':2}));
    // median curve  exp((mu-sig^2/2) t)
    let dmd=''; for(let i=0;i<=N;i++){ const t=i/N*T; dmd+=(i===0?'M':'L')+X(i)+','+Y(x_init*Math.exp((mu-0.5*sig*sig)*t))+' '; }
    svg.appendChild(SVG('path',{d:dmd,fill:'none',stroke:'var(--cyan)','stroke-width':2,'stroke-dasharray':'4 3'}));
    // legend
    const lg=SVG('g',{transform:'translate(80,40)'});
    function le(y,col,dash,lab){
      const ln=SVG('line',{x1:0,y1:y,x2:18,y2:y,stroke:col,'stroke-width':2});
      if(dash) ln.setAttribute('stroke-dasharray', dash);
      lg.appendChild(ln);
      const t=SVG('text',{x:24,y:y+4,'font-size':12,fill:col}); t.textContent=lab; lg.appendChild(t);
    }
    le(0,'var(--violet)',null,'sample paths');
    le(16,'var(--pink)',null,'mean  E[Xₜ] = x₀ eᵘᵗ');
    le(32,'var(--cyan)','4 3','median  x₀ exp((μ−σ²/2)t)');
    svg.appendChild(lg);
    // x ticks
    for(let k=0;k<=4;k++){
      const xp=X(k*N/4);
      svg.appendChild(SVG('line',{x1:xp,y1:y1,x2:xp,y2:y1+4,stroke:'var(--mute)'}));
      const t=SVG('text',{x:xp,y:y1+18,'text-anchor':'middle','font-size':10,fill:'var(--mute)'});
      t.textContent=(k/4*T).toFixed(1); svg.appendChild(t);
    }
    // empirical stats at t = T
    let s=0, ss=0;
    for(let k=0;k<K;k++){ const v=paths[k][N]; s+=v; ss+=v*v; }
    const m=s/K, va=ss/K - m*m;
    const meanTh=x_init*Math.exp(mu*T);
    const medTh=x_init*Math.exp((mu-0.5*sig*sig)*T);
    out.textContent =
      `dXₜ = μ Xₜ dt + σ Xₜ dBₜ,  X₀ = ${x_init},  T = ${T}\n`+
      `closed form:  Xₜ = X₀ exp((μ − σ²/2) t + σ Bₜ)\n\n`+
      `at t = T:   theory mean = ${meanTh.toFixed(3)},  theory median = ${medTh.toFixed(3)}\n`+
      `              gap (volatility drag) = ${(meanTh-medTh).toFixed(3)}\n`+
      `   empirical mean (K=${K})  = ${m.toFixed(3)},  empirical var = ${va.toFixed(3)}`;
  }
  muIn.addEventListener('input', draw);
  sigIn.addEventListener('input', draw);
  KIn.addEventListener('input', draw);
  $('#gbm-new').addEventListener('click',()=>{seed=null; draw();});
  draw();
})();
</script>
</section>

4. Girsanov's theorem and change of measure

Drift is measure-relative. Given a real-valued adapted process $\theta_s$ satisfying Novikov's condition $\mathbb{E}\exp(\tfrac{1}{2}\int_0^T\theta_s^2\,ds)<\infty$, define $$Z_t=\exp\!\Big(\int_0^t\theta_s\,dB_s-\tfrac{1}{2}\int_0^t\theta_s^2\,ds\Big).$$ By Itô on $\log Z$, $dZ_t=Z_t\,\theta_t\,dB_t$ — a strict Itô integral with no drift, hence $Z$ is a positive martingale with $\mathbb{E}[Z_t]=1$. Use $Z_T$ to define a new probability measure $\mathbb{Q}$ on $\mathcal{F}_T$ by $d\mathbb{Q}=Z_T\,d\mathbb{P}$. Girsanov's theorem says that under $\mathbb{Q}$, $$\tilde B_t = B_t - \int_0^t\theta_s\,ds$$ is a standard Brownian motion. The drift $\int\theta\,ds$ has been absorbed into the change of measure.

Risk-neutral pricing. If the asset solves $dS_t=\mu S_t\,dt+\sigma S_t\,dB_t$ under the real-world measure $\mathbb{P}$, set $\theta=(\mu-r)/\sigma$ (the market price of risk). Under $\mathbb{Q}$, $$dS_t = \mu S_t\,dt + \sigma S_t(d\tilde B_t+\theta\,dt) = r S_t\,dt + \sigma S_t\,d\tilde B_t,$$ so the discounted asset $e^{-rt}S_t$ becomes a martingale. Volatility $\sigma$ is unchanged; only the drift shifts. This is why the Black–Scholes price doesn't depend on $\mu$.

The widget below shifts mass between two measures via a single $\theta$ slider: same Brownian path, two laws of $B_T$. The base law is $\mathcal{N}(0,T)$; under $\mathbb{Q}$ the law of $B_T$ becomes $\mathcal{N}(\theta T, T)$ — equivalently, $\tilde B_T=B_T-\theta T\sim\mathcal{N}(0,T)$.

<script>
/* §4 — Girsanov density-shift visualization */
(function(){
  const svg=$('#gir-svg'), out=$('#gir-out');
  const thIn=$('#gir-th'), TIn=$('#gir-T');
  const thV=$('#gir-thv'), TV=$('#gir-Tv');
  const W=760, H=300, x0=60, x1=W-30, y0=30, y1=H-40;
  function phi(x,m,v){ return Math.exp(-((x-m)*(x-m))/(2*v))/Math.sqrt(2*Math.PI*v); }
  function draw(){
    const th=+thIn.value, T=+TIn.value;
    thV.textContent='θ = '+th.toFixed(2);

    svg.innerHTML='';

    const sd=Math.sqrt(T);
    const xmin=Math.min(-3*sd, th*T-3*sd) - 0.3;
    const xmax=Math.max( 3*sd, th*T+3*sd) + 0.3;
    const ymax=phi(0,0,T)*1.15;

    svg.appendChild(SVG('line',{x1:x0,y1:y1,x2:x1,y2:y1,stroke:'var(--line)'}));
    svg.appendChild(SVG('line',{x1:x0,y1:y0,x2:x0,y2:y1,stroke:'var(--line)'}));
    // P density (centered)
    let dP='', dQ='';
    const STEPS=200;
    for(let i=0;i<=STEPS;i++){
      const x=xmin+(i/STEPS)*(xmax-xmin);
      const yP=phi(x,0,T);
      const yQ=phi(x,th*T,T);
      dP += (i===0?'M':'L')+X(x)+','+Y(yP)+' ';
      dQ += (i===0?'M':'L')+X(x)+','+Y(yQ)+' ';

    // fill under P slightly

    svg.appendChild(SVG('path',{d:dP,fill:'none',stroke:'var(--yellow)','stroke-width':2}));
    svg.appendChild(SVG('path',{d:dQ,fill:'none',stroke:'var(--cyan)','stroke-width':2}));
    // shift arrow at peaks

    // x ticks

      const v=xmin+(k/4)*(xmax-xmin);

      lg.appendChild(SVG('line',{x1:0,y1:y,x2:18,y2:y,stroke:col,'stroke-width':2}));
      const t=SVG('text',{x:24,y:y+4,'font-size':12,fill:col}); t.textContent=lab; lg.appendChild(t);
    }
    le(0,'var(--yellow)','Bᴛ ~ 𝒩(0, T)   under ℙ');
    le(16,'var(--cyan)', 'Bᴛ ~ 𝒩(θT, T)  under ℚ');
    svg.appendChild(lg);
    const cap=SVG('text',{x:W/2,y:20,'text-anchor':'middle','font-size':12,fill:'var(--mute)'});
    cap.textContent='dℚ/dℙ = exp(θ Bᴛ − ½θ²T) — same path, different mass';
    svg.appendChild(cap);
    // readout
    const ZbarMean = 1; // E_P[Z_T] = 1 by martingale prop
    const tildeMean = 0; // E_Q[ B_T - θT ] = 0
    out.textContent =
      `density:  Zᴛ = exp(θ Bᴛ − ½θ²T),  𝔼ℙ[Zᴛ] = ${ZbarMean}\n`+
      `under ℚ:  𝔼ℚ[Bᴛ] = θT = ${(th*T).toFixed(3)},  𝔼ℚ[Bᴛ − θT] = ${tildeMean}\n`+
      `Brownian motion under ℚ:  ̃Bₜ = Bₜ − θ t  (drift absorbed into the measure)`;
  }
  thIn.addEventListener('input', draw);
  TIn.addEventListener('input', draw);
  draw();
})();
</script>

5. Feynman–Kac formula

Itô gives a direct dictionary between SDEs and second-order parabolic PDEs. Suppose $u(t,x)\in C^{1,2}$ solves the backward heat-type equation $$\partial_t u + b(x)\partial_x u + \tfrac{1}{2}\sigma(x)^2\partial_{xx}u - r(x)u = 0,\qquad u(T,x)=g(x),$$ and let $X_t$ be the diffusion with drift $b$ and volatility $\sigma$. Apply Itô to $e^{-\int_0^s r\,dr}u(s,X_s)$: the $dt$ pieces cancel by the PDE, leaving a martingale. Taking expectations gives the Feynman–Kac formula: $$u(t,x)=\mathbb{E}\!\left[e^{-\int_t^T r(X_s)\,ds}\,g(X_T)\;\Big|\;X_t=x\right].$$ The PDE evaluation problem reduces to averaging over Brownian paths.

The simplest case. With $b=0$, $\sigma=1$, $r=0$ this is just $\partial_t u+\tfrac{1}{2}\partial_{xx}u=0$, $u(T,x)=g(x)$ — the backward heat equation solved by Brownian convolution: $$u(t,x)=\mathbb{E}[g(B_T)\mid B_t=x]=\int_{-\infty}^\infty g(y)\,\frac{e^{-(y-x)^2/2(T-t)}}{\sqrt{2\pi(T-t)}}\,dy.$$ The Brownian heat kernel is the Green's function. The widget below draws the heat-equation solution at a slider time $t$, both as the analytic Gaussian convolution (yellow) and as a Monte-Carlo estimate over Brownian paths (cyan dots) — visually the law of large numbers walking the PDE forward.

Heat-equation solution as Brownian expectation
$u(t,x)=\mathbb{E}[g(B_T)\mid B_t=x]$ · $g$ a bump
t = 0.30 (T = 1) M = 400

<script>
/* §5 — Feynman–Kac: heat equation = Brownian expectation, with MC overlay */
(function(){
  const svg=$('#fk-svg'), out=$('#fk-out');
  const tIn=$('#fk-t'), MIn=$('#fk-M');
  const tV=$('#fk-tv'), MV=$('#fk-Mv');
  const W=760, H=320, x0=60, x1=W-30, y0=30, y1=H-40;
  const T=1.0;
  // terminal condition g(x): a smooth bump centered at 1
  function g(y){ return Math.exp(-2*(y-1)*(y-1)); }
  // analytic u(t,x) = E[g(B_T)|B_t=x] = ∫ g(y) phi(y; x, T-t) dy
  function uAnalytic(t,x){
    const v=T-t;
    if(v<=1e-6) return g(x);
    let s=0;
    const N=200, ymin=-4, ymax=4;
    for(let i=0;i<=N;i++){
      const y=ymin+(i/N)*(ymax-ymin);
      const w=(i===0||i===N)?0.5:1;
      const k=Math.exp(-((y-x)*(y-x))/(2*v))/Math.sqrt(2*Math.PI*v);
      s += w * g(y) * k;
    }
    return s * (ymax-ymin)/N;
  }
  // MC: for each x, simulate M Brownian increments to T-t, average g(x + sqrt(T-t) Z)
  let mcCache=null;
  function uMC(t,xs,M){
    const v=T-t;
    if(v<=0) return xs.map(g);
    if(!mcCache || mcCache.length<M){
      mcCache=new Float64Array(M);
      for(let k=0;k<M;k++) mcCache[k]=randn();
    }
    return xs.map(x=>{
      let s=0;
      for(let k=0;k<M;k++) s += g(x + Math.sqrt(v)*mcCache[k]);
      return s/M;
    });
  }
  function draw(){
    const t=+tIn.value, M=+MIn.value;
    tV.textContent=`t = ${t.toFixed(2)} (T = ${T.toFixed(0)})`;
    MV.textContent='M = '+M;
    svg.innerHTML='';
    // x range
    const xmin=-2.5, xmax=4.5, ymin=0, ymax=1.05;
    const X=v=>x0+(v-xmin)/(xmax-xmin)*(x1-x0);
    const Y=v=>y1-(v-ymin)/(ymax-ymin)*(y1-y0);
    // axes
    svg.appendChild(SVG('line',{x1:x0,y1:y1,x2:x1,y2:y1,stroke:'var(--line)'}));
    svg.appendChild(SVG('line',{x1:x0,y1:y0,x2:x0,y2:y1,stroke:'var(--line)'}));
    // grid for x
    const xs=[];
    const SN=200;
    for(let i=0;i<=SN;i++) xs.push(xmin+(i/SN)*(xmax-xmin));
    // analytic u(t,x)
    let dU='';
    xs.forEach((x,i)=>{ dU += (i===0?'M':'L')+X(x)+','+Y(uAnalytic(t,x))+' '; });
    svg.appendChild(SVG('path',{d:dU,fill:'none',stroke:'var(--yellow)','stroke-width':2}));
    // terminal g(x) faint
    let dg='';
    xs.forEach((x,i)=>{ dg += (i===0?'M':'L')+X(x)+','+Y(g(x))+' '; });
    svg.appendChild(SVG('path',{d:dg,fill:'none',stroke:'var(--mute)','stroke-width':1.2,'stroke-dasharray':'3 3'}));
    // MC dots at sparser x
    const xsMC=[];
    for(let i=0;i<=20;i++) xsMC.push(xmin+(i/20)*(xmax-xmin));
    const uMCv = uMC(t, xsMC, M);
    xsMC.forEach((x,i)=>{
      svg.appendChild(SVG('circle',{cx:X(x),cy:Y(uMCv[i]),r:3,fill:'var(--cyan)'}));
    });
    // ticks
    for(let k=0;k<=6;k++){
      const v=xmin+(k/6)*(xmax-xmin);
      const xp=X(v);
      svg.appendChild(SVG('line',{x1:xp,y1:y1,x2:xp,y2:y1+4,stroke:'var(--mute)'}));
      const tt=SVG('text',{x:xp,y:y1+18,'text-anchor':'middle','font-size':10,fill:'var(--mute)'});
      tt.textContent=v.toFixed(1); svg.appendChild(tt);
    }
    // legend (viewport)
    const lg=SVG('g',{transform:'translate(80,40)'});
    function le(y,col,dash,lab){
      const ln=SVG('line',{x1:0,y1:y,x2:18,y2:y,stroke:col,'stroke-width':2});
      if(dash) ln.setAttribute('stroke-dasharray', dash);
      lg.appendChild(ln);
      const t=SVG('text',{x:24,y:y+4,'font-size':12,fill:col}); t.textContent=lab; lg.appendChild(t);
    }
    le(0,'var(--yellow)',null,'u(t,x) — heat-equation solution');
    le(16,'var(--cyan)',null,'Monte-Carlo Brownian average');
    le(32,'var(--mute)','3 3','g(x) terminal data');
    svg.appendChild(lg);
    const cap=SVG('text',{x:W/2,y:20,'text-anchor':'middle','font-size':12,fill:'var(--mute)'});
    cap.textContent='backward heat:  ∂ₜu + ½ ∂ₓₓu = 0,  u(T,x) = g(x) = exp(−2(x−1)²)';
    svg.appendChild(cap);
    // readout — sample at x = 1
    const xstar=1.0;
    const aA=uAnalytic(t,xstar);
    const aM = uMC(t, [xstar], M)[0];
    out.textContent =
      `at t = ${t.toFixed(2)}, x = 1:\n`+
      `  analytic u(t,1)  = ${aA.toFixed(4)}\n`+
      `  Monte-Carlo (M=${M}) = ${aM.toFixed(4)}\n\n`+
      `as t → T the curve collapses onto g(x); as t → 0 the bump diffuses outward.`;
  }
  tIn.addEventListener('input', draw);
  MIn.addEventListener('input',()=>{mcCache=null; draw();});
  $('#fk-new').addEventListener('click',()=>{mcCache=null; draw();});
  draw();
})();
</script>
</section>

6. Applications: Black–Scholes, Kalman, optimal stopping

The three pieces — Itô's formula, SDE solutions, Girsanov, Feynman–Kac — assemble into the workhorses of mathematical finance, signal processing, and decision theory.

Black–Scholes

Under the risk-neutral measure $\mathbb{Q}$, $S_T = S_0\exp((r-\sigma^2/2)T+\sigma\tilde B_T)$. The price of a European call $C=e^{-rT}\mathbb{E}_\mathbb{Q}[(S_T-K)^+]$ admits a closed form $$C = S_0\,\Phi(d_1) - K e^{-rT}\,\Phi(d_2),\qquad d_{1,2} = \frac{\log(S_0/K) + (r\pm\sigma^2/2)T}{\sigma\sqrt T}.$$ Equivalently, $C$ solves the Black–Scholes PDE $\partial_t C+\tfrac{1}{2}\sigma^2 S^2\partial_{SS}C+rS\,\partial_S C-rC=0$ with terminal $C(T,S)=(S-K)^+$. The widget below visualises the discrete-time binomial tree that converges (under the right parameters) to Black–Scholes.

Kalman filter

For the linear-Gaussian state-space SDE $dX_t=A X_t\,dt+\sigma\,dB_t$ with linear noisy observation $Y_t$, the conditional distribution of $X_t$ given $\mathcal{F}_t^Y$ remains Gaussian. The conditional mean $\hat X_t=\mathbb{E}[X_t\mid\mathcal{F}_t^Y]$ and covariance $P_t$ obey the closed Kalman–Bucy ODEs — recursive, online, and exact. This is the canonical example of conditional expectations as an orthogonal projection.

Optimal stopping

An American option pays $g(X_\tau)$ at any stopping time $\tau\in[0,T]$ chosen by the holder, so $$V(t,x)=\sup_{\tau\in[t,T]}\mathbb{E}_\mathbb{Q}\!\left[e^{-r(\tau-t)}g(X_\tau)\,\big|\,X_t=x\right].$$ $V$ solves a variational PDE: $\min(\,-\partial_t V-\mathcal{L}V+rV,\;V-g\,)=0$, with a free boundary separating the continuation region (where the PDE part is active) from the exercise region (where $V=g$). This pattern — Brownian-driven SDE, Bellman/HJB, free boundary — is the prototype for stochastic control.

Binomial tree → Black–Scholes
$N$-step CRR tree backward-induced
N = 5 K = 100 σ = 0.25

<script>
/* §6 — CRR binomial tree visualization with backward induction */
(function(){
  const svg=$('#bs-svg'), out=$('#bs-out');
  const NIn=$('#bs-N'), KIn=$('#bs-K'), sigIn=$('#bs-sig');
  const NV=$('#bs-Nv'), KV=$('#bs-Kv'), sigV=$('#bs-sigv');
  const W=760, H=360, x0=60, x1=W-50, y0=30, y1=H-30;
  const S0=100, T=1.0, r=0.05;
  function bsCall(S,K,r,sig,T){
    if(T<=0) return Math.max(S-K,0);
    const d1=(Math.log(S/K)+(r+0.5*sig*sig)*T)/(sig*Math.sqrt(T));
    const d2=d1-sig*Math.sqrt(T);
    function Phi(x){
      // Abramowitz–Stegun 7.1.26
      const sign=x<0?-1:1; x=Math.abs(x)/Math.SQRT2;
      const a1=0.254829592,a2=-0.284496736,a3=1.421413741,a4=-1.453152027,a5=1.061405429,p=0.3275911;
      const t=1/(1+p*x);
      const y=1-(((((a5*t+a4)*t)+a3)*t+a2)*t+a1)*t*Math.exp(-x*x);
      return 0.5*(1+sign*y);
    }
    return S*Phi(d1) - K*Math.exp(-r*T)*Phi(d2);
  }
  function draw(){
    const N=+NIn.value, K=+KIn.value, sig=+sigIn.value;
    NV.textContent='N = '+N;
    KV.textContent='K = '+K;
    sigV.textContent='σ = '+sig.toFixed(2);
    svg.innerHTML='';
    const dt=T/N;
    const u=Math.exp(sig*Math.sqrt(dt));
    const d=1/u;
    const p=(Math.exp(r*dt)-d)/(u-d);
    // build stock tree S[i][j], j = number of ups, i = step
    const S=[]; for(let i=0;i<=N;i++){ S.push([]); for(let j=0;j<=i;j++) S[i].push(S0*Math.pow(u,j)*Math.pow(d,i-j)); }
    // option tree by backward induction
    const C=[]; for(let i=0;i<=N;i++) C.push(new Array(i+1).fill(0));
    for(let j=0;j<=N;j++) C[N][j]=Math.max(S[N][j]-K,0);
    for(let i=N-1;i>=0;i--){
      for(let j=0;j<=i;j++){
        C[i][j] = Math.exp(-r*dt)*(p*C[i+1][j+1] + (1-p)*C[i+1][j]);
      }
    }
    // layout: each step at fixed x, nodes vertically spread
    const X=i=>x0 + (i/N)*(x1-x0);
    const Y=(i,j)=>{
      // center at (y0+y1)/2; spread half-height = (y1-y0)/2 - 14
      const half=(y1-y0)/2 - 18;
      return (y0+y1)/2 - (j - i/2)*(half*2/Math.max(N,1));
    };
    // edges
    for(let i=0;i<N;i++){
      for(let j=0;j<=i;j++){
        const a=[X(i),Y(i,j)];
        const bUp=[X(i+1),Y(i+1,j+1)];
        const bDn=[X(i+1),Y(i+1,j)];
        svg.appendChild(SVG('line',{x1:a[0],y1:a[1],x2:bUp[0],y2:bUp[1],stroke:'var(--green)','stroke-width':1,'stroke-opacity':0.55}));
        svg.appendChild(SVG('line',{x1:a[0],y1:a[1],x2:bDn[0],y2:bDn[1],stroke:'var(--pink)','stroke-width':1,'stroke-opacity':0.55}));
      }
    }
    // nodes
    for(let i=0;i<=N;i++){
      for(let j=0;j<=i;j++){
        const cx=X(i), cy=Y(i,j);
        const itm = S[i][j] > K;
        svg.appendChild(SVG('circle',{cx,cy,r:13, fill:'var(--panel2)', stroke: itm?'var(--green)':'var(--mute)','stroke-width':1.2}));
        const t1=SVG('text',{x:cx,y:cy-1,'text-anchor':'middle','font-size':9,fill:'var(--ink)'});
        t1.textContent='S='+S[i][j].toFixed(0);
        svg.appendChild(t1);
        const t2=SVG('text',{x:cx,y:cy+9,'text-anchor':'middle','font-size':9,fill:'var(--cyan)'});
        t2.textContent='C='+C[i][j].toFixed(2);
        svg.appendChild(t2);
      }
    }
    // axis labels
    for(let i=0;i<=N;i++){
      const xp=X(i);
      const t=SVG('text',{x:xp,y:y1+16,'text-anchor':'middle','font-size':10,fill:'var(--mute)'});
      t.textContent='t='+ (i*dt).toFixed(2);
      svg.appendChild(t);
    }
    const cap=SVG('text',{x:W/2,y:20,'text-anchor':'middle','font-size':12,fill:'var(--mute)'});
    cap.textContent=`CRR tree:  u = e^(σ√Δt) = ${u.toFixed(3)},  d = 1/u = ${d.toFixed(3)},  p = ${p.toFixed(3)}`;
    svg.appendChild(cap);
    const treePrice = C[0][0];
    const bsPrice = bsCall(S0,K,r,sig,T);
    out.textContent =
      `parameters:  S₀ = ${S0},  K = ${K},  r = ${r},  σ = ${sig.toFixed(2)},  T = ${T.toFixed(1)}\n`+
      `tree price (N = ${N}):  C₀ = ${treePrice.toFixed(4)}\n`+
      `Black–Scholes limit:    C₀ = ${bsPrice.toFixed(4)}\n`+
      `relative error:          ${(Math.abs(treePrice-bsPrice)/bsPrice*100).toFixed(2)} %`;
  }
  NIn.addEventListener('input', draw);
  KIn.addEventListener('input', draw);
  sigIn.addEventListener('input', draw);
  draw();
})();
</script>
</section>

7. Connections

Itô calculus is the bridge between probability and analysis — once $dB^2 = dt$ is internalised, three classes of theorems start to feel inevitable rather than mysterious.

Stochastic processes and martingales

The discrete and continuous-time martingale theory is the structural backbone — Doob decomposition, optional stopping, BDG inequalities — on which the Itô integral is built. The integrand class (predictable, locally square-integrable) is exactly the language we used there.

Partial differential equations

Feynman–Kac is one face of a deep symmetry: parabolic PDEs and continuous-time martingales are two ways to look at the same flow of probability mass. The heat equation and harmonic-function theory reappear as expectations, and conversely PDE methods (maximum principles, semigroups) attack stochastic problems.

Mathematical finance

Black–Scholes is a self-financing-replication argument plus Itô's formula plus an SDE for the underlying. The change of measure that turns the discounted price into a martingale (the risk-neutral measure) is exactly the Girsanov transform of $\S 4$. Beyond European options, jump-diffusion, stochastic volatility, and interest-rate term-structure models are all formulated this way.

Statistical mechanics and SPDEs

Fluctuation theory of interacting particle systems is the source of stochastic PDEs (KPZ, Allen–Cahn, the stochastic heat equation). Roughness theory (Hairer, Gubinelli) extends Itô calculus to drivers rougher than Brownian motion.

Open frontiers: rough-path theory and regularity structures (Hairer's Fields-medal work on KPZ); Malliavin calculus as a calculus of variations on Wiener space; stochastic optimal transport and entropic regularization, which connect Itô calculus to large deviations and the Schrödinger problem.
